Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Comparative adjectival phrase with choby + noun phrase + locative prepositional phrase; fixed simile used predicatively or adverbially.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone looks awkward, confused, out of place, or visibly uncomfortable in a situation they do not understand.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: wyglōndać niypewnie abo niy pasować do sytuacyje
Usage Contexts: daily speech; family teasing; school; humorous descriptions of embarrassment
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Built on rural-comic imagery: an animal in a kitchen or food setting creates an absurd picture of helplessness and mismatch, typical of Upper Silesian household humor.
First Example Sentence: Kej go posadzili przi stole ze starzikami, siedziŏł choby żaba w giskanie.
Second Example Sentence: Na piyrszyj szychcie w biurze Antek wyglōndoł choby żaba w giskanie.
Third Example Sentence: Niy stōj tak choby żaba w giskanie, yno wejź do izby i przidej sie do godki.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed proverbial clause; plural noun phrase + future verb form + plural result noun; predictive warning structure.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A harmless-looking game, prank, or risky behavior may soon end badly, especially with children.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: z zabawy może być płacz abo kłopot
Usage Contexts: family speech; children; warnings; playground; domestic advice
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: A household proverb often directed at children; it reflects a pragmatic Silesian style of warning before a small accident happens.
First Example Sentence: Bajtle, niy skokejcie po werszalce, bo z graczkōw przidōm płaczki.
Second Example Sentence: Mama zaroz pedziała, że z graczkōw przidōm płaczki, kej ujrzała te rolki na schodach.
Third Example Sentence: Ôni sie śmiali, ale jo już wiedzioł, że z tych graczkōw przidōm płaczki.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitival verbal idiom; transitive verb + concrete object + prepositional complement; can be conjugated by subject and tense.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To deceive someone, invent excuses, or tell an unbelievable story while expecting the listener to accept it.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: cyganić, ôszukiwać abo wciskać komuś niyprawda
Usage Contexts: teenage excuses; workplace; politics; negotiation; skeptical replies
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: A food-based metaphor close to Central European comic imagery: impossible physical placement marks a statement as obviously fabricated.
First Example Sentence: Niy wiysz mi nudli na uszy, bo wiym, kaj żeś wczora bōł.
Second Example Sentence: Szef prōbowoł nōm wiyszać nudle na uszy, że premije przidōm dopiyro po wakacyjach.
Third Example Sentence: Jak polityk tak ôbiycuje bez liczbōw, to ludziom yno wiyszo nudle na uszy.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Imperfective verbal simile; verb + jak + occupational noun; often used in second person for direct criticism.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To talk nonsense, ramble incoherently, or speak in a way that lacks logic and substance.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: godać gupoty abo niytrzymać sie rzeczy
Usage Contexts: arguments; peer talk; humorous correction; family disputes; online comments
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses the figure of a worker associated with spreading material unevenly; the phrase humorously maps messy physical work onto messy speech.
First Example Sentence: Przestōń fulać jak mulorz i pedz jasno, czego chcesz.
Second Example Sentence: Na zebraniu jedyn fuloł jak mulorz, a reszta cicho patrzała na zygor.
Third Example Sentence: Kej niy mosz faktōw, to niy fulej jak mulorz przi cołkij klasie.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Possessive verbal idiom with mieć + concrete object + locative prepositional phrase; usually conjugated for present or past states.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be reluctant to work, move slowly, or act as if every task is too heavy.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: być lyniwy abo niy mieć chynci do roboty
Usage Contexts: workplace; chores; family reminders; jokes about laziness
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The hand image fits Silesian industrial and household work culture, where useful hands symbolize readiness, skill, and responsibility.
First Example Sentence: Weź sie za łopata, bo niy bydymy czekać, aż ci tyn piosek z rynkōw wyleci.
Second Example Sentence: Ôd rana mosz piosek w rynkowach, a szrank dali stoji niyposprzōntany.
Third Example Sentence: Na grubie niy szło mieć piosku w rynkowach, bo kożdy mioł swoja robota.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Imperfective verbal idiom with object + temporal prepositional phrase; adaptable across persons, tenses, and domains.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To do something at the very last moment, just before a deadline or consequence.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: robić coś na sam kōniec abo za niyskoro
Usage Contexts: school; university; administration; workplace deadlines; everyday planning
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The idiom is linked to the pressure of a final push, resonating with punctuality norms in industrial and office routines.
First Example Sentence: Referat zaś robiōm na ôstatni driker, a potym dziwiōm sie, że brakuje ôbrŏzkōw.
Second Example Sentence: Niy zostowiej podpisu na ôstatni driker, bo urzōnd zamyko ô trzeci.
Third Example Sentence: Jak wszyjsko robisz na ôstatni driker, to kożdŏ środa wyglōndŏ jak alarm.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Second-person present clause with comparative abundance phrase; formulaic reassurance or mild correction.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: There is plenty of time; the person should not panic or rush unnecessarily.
Usage Contexts: daily planning; travel; family; calming someone down before an event
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Maras evokes a plentiful, messy mass; the phrase exaggerates abundance in earthy Silesian imagery.
First Example Sentence: Niy nerwuj sie przed banōm, mosz czasu jak marasu.
Second Example Sentence: Do wesela jeszcze dwa tydnie, mosz czasu jak marasu na kupiynie galot.
Third Example Sentence: Ôpa sie zaś śmioł, że mosz czasu jak marasu, a i tak lecisz bez sznōrowek.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal idiom; noun + directional prepositional phrase; functions as a label for a type of conversation.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Empty, casual, or low-stakes talk that fills time but does not settle anything important.
Usage Contexts: small talk; workplace; neighborhood; waiting rooms; social observation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Szkubaczka refers to feather-plucking gatherings and domestic communal work, where conversation accompanied repetitive manual tasks.
First Example Sentence: Przi ekspresie do kafy była yno godka ku szkubaczce, ale atmosfera zaroz sie polepszyła.
Second Example Sentence: Po szychcie chłopy zaczli godka ku szkubaczce ô pogodzie, fusbalu i nowych światłach w Bytōmiu.
Third Example Sentence: Ôna niy lubi zebrań, kaj godzina idzie na godka ku szkubaczce, a decyzyje niy ma.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Adjectival noun phrase; adjective + noun; can be modified, intensified, or used predicatively.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Speech full of barbed remarks, indirect criticism, sarcasm, or cleverly hidden attacks.
Usage Contexts: family disputes; neighbors; politics; satire; workplace tension
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The metaphor of sharpness turns social tension into a physical point or needle; this fits Silesian indirect humor and guarded criticism.
First Example Sentence: Przi niedzielnym ôbiedzie szpicatŏ godka leciała cicho, ale kożdy wiedzioł, do kogo trefiŏ.
Second Example Sentence: Redaktōr napisoł taki felietōn, że szpicatŏ godka ô radnych bolała jeszcze po tydniu.
Third Example Sentence: Miyndzy tymi sōmsiadkami niy ma wojny, ale szpicatŏ godka idzie bez płot jak igła.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negated possessive existential idiom; niy + mieć + quantifier phrase + locative complement; fixed evaluative clause.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To behave irrationally, say foolish things, or act as though one lacks common sense.
Usage Contexts: heated conversation; humorous criticism; warning; gossip; reactions to strange behavior
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The house metaphor presents the mind as a household whose members should all be present; absence signals disorder or poor judgment.
First Example Sentence: Czy ty niy mosz wszyjskich w doma, że chcesz jechać na kole bez lampy po ćmoku?
Second Example Sentence: Ôni pedzieli, że skasujōm archiwum bez kopije, jakby niy mieli wszyjskich w doma.
Third Example Sentence: Niy gŏdej, że starzik niy mioł wszyjskich w doma, bo ôn yno sprawdzoł, czy bajtle słuchajōm.
